import pytest
from order_domain import Money, Order, OrderLine, OrderStatus
from order_total import payable_total


def test_total_and_empty_order():
    order = Order("A-1", OrderStatus.CONFIRMED, [OrderLine("x", 2, Money(10)), OrderLine("y", 1, Money(5))])
    assert payable_total(order, 0.1) == Money(27.5, "USD")
    assert payable_total(Order("A-2", OrderStatus.DRAFT, [], "EUR"), 0) == Money(0, "EUR")


def test_invalid_orders():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="cancelled"):
        payable_total(Order("A-3", OrderStatus.CANCELLED), 0.1)
    mixed = Order("A-4", OrderStatus.CONFIRMED, [OrderLine("x", 1, Money(1, "USD")), OrderLine("y", 1, Money(1, "EUR"))])
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="mixed"):
        payable_total(mixed, 0)

target_source:
from billing_models import DiscountBand, Invoice, InvoiceSummary


def summarize_invoice(invoice: Invoice, band: DiscountBand) -> InvoiceSummary:
    subtotal = sum(item.quantity * item.unit_price for item in invoice.items)
    discount = subtotal * band.rate if subtotal >= band.minimum_subtotal else 0.0
    taxable = sum(item.quantity * item.unit_price for item in invoice.items if item.taxable)
    tax = taxable * invoice.tax_rate
    return InvoiceSummary(round(subtotal, 2), round(discount, 2), round(tax, 2), round(subtotal - discount + tax, 2))
reference_tests:
from billing_models import DiscountBand, Invoice, InvoiceItem, InvoiceSummary
from invoice_pricing import summarize_invoice


def test_discount_and_tax_contract():
    invoice = Invoice("c1", (InvoiceItem("a", 2, 30), InvoiceItem("b", 1, 50, False)), 0.1)
    assert summarize_invoice(invoice, DiscountBand(100, 0.2)) == InvoiceSummary(110, 22, 6, 94)


def test_below_discount_threshold():
    invoice = Invoice("c2", (InvoiceItem("a", 1, 20),), 0.05)
    assert summarize_invoice(invoice, DiscountBand(100, 0.2)) == InvoiceSummary(20, 0, 1, 21)

from access_domain import Permission, Role, User
from permission_policy import can_access


def test_role_and_owner_permissions():
    reader = User("u1", (Role("reader", frozenset({Permission.READ})),))
    assert can_access(reader, Permission.READ) is True
    assert can_access(reader, Permission.EDIT_ANY) is False
    assert can_access(reader, Permission.EDIT_OWN, "u1") is True
    assert can_access(reader, Permission.EDIT_OWN, "u2") is False


def test_suspension_overrides_grant():
    admin = User("u2", (Role("admin", frozenset({Permission.EDIT_ANY})),), True)
    assert can_access(admin, Permission.EDIT_ANY) is False

target_source:
from tree_domain import NodeKind, TreeNode


def build_manifest(root: TreeNode) -> list[str]:
    result: list[str] = []

    def visit(node: TreeNode, prefix: str) -> None:
        path = f"{prefix}/{node.name}" if prefix else node.name
        marker = "/" if node.kind is NodeKind.DIRECTORY else ""
        result.append(path + marker)
        for child in sorted(node.children, key=lambda item: item.name):
            visit(child, path)

    visit(root, "")
    return result
reference_tests:
from tree_domain import NodeKind, TreeNode
from tree_manifest import build_manifest


def test_nested_manifest_is_sorted():
    root = TreeNode("root", NodeKind.DIRECTORY)
    src = TreeNode("src", NodeKind.DIRECTORY).add(TreeNode("main.py", NodeKind.FILE))
    root.add(src).add(TreeNode("README.md", NodeKind.FILE))
    assert build_manifest(root) == ["root/", "root/README.md", "root/src/", "root/src/main.py"]


def test_file_root():
    assert build_manifest(TreeNode("one.txt", NodeKind.FILE)) == ["one.txt"]

import pytest
from user_lookup import active_email
from user_ports import UserNotFound, UserRecord


class Repo:
    def __init__(self, value): self.value = value; self.calls = []
    def get_by_id(self, identifier): self.calls.append(identifier); return self.value


def test_active_user_and_repository_contract():
    repo = Repo(UserRecord("7", "Ada@Example.COM"))
    assert active_email(repo, "7") == "ada@example.com"
    assert repo.calls == ["7"]


def test_missing_and_inactive_users():
    with pytest.raises(UserNotFound): active_email(Repo(None), "x")
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="inactive"): active_email(Repo(UserRecord("8", "x@y", False)), "8")

import pytest
from payment_ports import GatewayResponse, PaymentDeclined, PaymentRequest, PaymentResult
from payment_processor import collect_payment


class Gateway:
    def __init__(self, response): self.response = response; self.request = None
    def charge(self, request): self.request = request; return self.response


def test_approved_charge_uses_request_contract():
    gateway = Gateway(GatewayResponse(True, "tx-9"))
    assert collect_payment(gateway, "o-1", 1250, "tok") == PaymentResult("o-1", "tx-9", 1250)
    assert gateway.request == PaymentRequest("o-1", 1250, "tok")


def test_decline_and_invalid_amount():
    with pytest.raises(PaymentDeclined, match="blocked"): collect_payment(Gateway(GatewayResponse(False, reason="blocked")), "o", 1, "t")
    with pytest.raises(ValueError): collect_payment(Gateway(None), "o", 0, "t")

from catalog_ports import Product
from catalog_service import CatalogService


class Repo:
    def __init__(self, value): self.value = value; self.calls = []
    def find_by_sku(self, sku): self.calls.append(sku); return self.value


class Cache:
    def __init__(self, value=None): self.value = value; self.writes = []
    def read(self, key): return self.value
    def write(self, key, value, ttl_seconds): self.writes.append((key, value, ttl_seconds))


def test_cache_hit_skips_repository():
    repo = Repo(None); cache = Cache({"sku": "A", "title": "Apple", "price_cents": "99"})
    assert CatalogService(repo, cache).find("A") == Product("A", "Apple", 99)
    assert repo.calls == []


def test_cache_miss_writes_product():
    product = Product("B", "Book", 500); repo = Repo(product); cache = Cache()
    assert CatalogService(repo, cache).find("B") == product
    assert cache.writes == [("product:B", product.to_mapping(), 60)]

import json
import pytest
from app_config import AppConfig, Environment
from json_config_loader import load_config


def test_loads_schema_and_defaults(tmp_path):
    path = tmp_path / "config.json"; path.write_text(json.dumps({"endpoint": "https://api.test/"}), encoding="utf-8")
    assert load_config(path) == AppConfig("https://api.test", Environment.DEVELOPMENT, 15)


def test_explicit_values_and_bad_endpoint(tmp_path):
    path = tmp_path / "config.json"; path.write_text(json.dumps({"endpoint": "http://local", "environment": "staging", "timeout_seconds": "8"}), encoding="utf-8")
    assert load_config(path) == AppConfig("http://local", Environment.STAGING, 8)
    path.write_text(json.dumps({"endpoint": "ftp://bad"}), encoding="utf-8")
    with pytest.raises(ValueError): load_config(path)

target_source:
import csv
from pathlib import Path

from customer_schema import CustomerRecord, CustomerTier, REQUIRED_COLUMNS


def import_customers(path: str | Path) -> list[CustomerRecord]:
    with Path(path).open(newline="", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
        reader = csv.DictReader(handle)
        if not REQUIRED_COLUMNS.issubset(reader.fieldnames or []):
            raise ValueError("missing columns")
        return [CustomerRecord(row["customer_id"], row["name"].strip(), CustomerTier(row["tier"].lower())) for row in reader]

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="missing columns"): import_customers(path)

target_source:
import csv
from pathlib import Path

from report_contracts import OutputPolicy, ReportRow


def export_report(rows: list[ReportRow], name: str, policy: OutputPolicy) -> Path:
    destination = policy.destination_for(name, suffix=".csv")
    destination.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with destination.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
        writer = csv.writer(handle)
        writer.writerow(ReportRow.columns())
        for row in rows:
            writer.writerow(row.as_cells())
    return destination
reference_tests:
from report_contracts import ReportRow
from report_exporter import export_report


class Policy:
    def __init__(self, root): self.root = root; self.calls = []
    def destination_for(self, name, suffix): self.calls.append((name, suffix)); return self.root / "nested" / f"{name}{suffix}"


def test_exports_using_project_contract(tmp_path):
    policy = Policy(tmp_path)
    result = export_report([ReportRow("ok", 3, 0.5)], "daily", policy)
    assert result == tmp_path / "nested" / "daily.csv"
    assert policy.calls == [("daily", ".csv")]
    assert result.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines() == ["label,count,ratio", "ok,3,0.50"]

import pytest
from deployment_contracts import DeploymentSettings, LogLevel
from environment_settings import parse_environment


def test_defaults_and_explicit_environment():
    assert parse_environment({}) == DeploymentSettings("127.0.0.1", 8080, False, LogLevel.INFO)
    assert parse_environment({"APP_HOST": " api " , "APP_PORT": "9000", "APP_DEBUG": "YES", "APP_LOG_LEVEL": "WARNING"}) == DeploymentSettings("api", 9000, True, LogLevel.WARNING)


def test_invalid_port():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="invalid port"): parse_environment({"APP_PORT": "70000"})

from rate_contracts import RateLimitDecision, RateLimitPolicy
from window_limiter import WindowRateLimiter


class Clock:
    def __init__(self, now=10.0): self.now = now
    def monotonic(self): return self.now


def test_state_and_window_expiration():
    clock = Clock(); limiter = WindowRateLimiter(RateLimitPolicy(2, 5), clock)
    assert limiter.check("u") == RateLimitDecision(True, 1, None)
    clock.now = 11; assert limiter.check("u") == RateLimitDecision(True, 0, None)
    clock.now = 12; assert limiter.check("u") == RateLimitDecision(False, 0, 15.0)
    clock.now = 15; assert limiter.check("u") == RateLimitDecision(True, 0, None)


def test_keys_are_independent():
    clock = Clock(); limiter = WindowRateLimiter(RateLimitPolicy(1, 10), clock)
    assert limiter.check("a").allowed and limiter.check("b").allowed

import pytest
from inventory_domain import InsufficientStock, Reservation, StockItem
from inventory_service import InventoryService


def test_reserve_is_idempotent_and_release_restores_stock():
    item = StockItem("A", 5); service = InventoryService([item])
    expected = Reservation("r1", "A", 3)
    assert service.reserve("r1", "A", 3) == expected
    assert service.reserve("r1", "A", 3) == expected and item.available == 2
    assert service.release("r1") is True and item.available == 5
    assert service.release("r1") is False


def test_invalid_and_insufficient_quantities():
    service = InventoryService([StockItem("A", 1)])
    with pytest.raises(ValueError): service.reserve("r", "A", 0)
    with pytest.raises(InsufficientStock): service.reserve("r", "A", 2)

from event_contracts import Event
from event_dispatcher import EventDispatcher


class Listener:
    def __init__(self): self.events = []
    def handle(self, event): self.events.append(event)


def test_subscribe_deduplicates_and_dispatches():
    dispatcher = EventDispatcher(); listener = Listener(); event = Event("saved", {"id": 1})
    dispatcher.subscribe("saved", listener); dispatcher.subscribe("saved", listener)
    assert dispatcher.dispatch(event) == 1 and listener.events == [event]


def test_unsubscribe_contract():
    dispatcher = EventDispatcher(); listener = Listener(); dispatcher.subscribe("x", listener)
    assert dispatcher.unsubscribe("x", listener) is True
    assert dispatcher.unsubscribe("x", listener) is False
    assert dispatcher.dispatch(Event("x")) == 0

import asyncio

from async_user_ports import AsyncUserRepository, UserRecord


async def active_display_names(repository: AsyncUserRepository, identifiers: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    users = await asyncio.gather(*(repository.fetch(identifier) for identifier in identifiers))
    return [user.display_name for user in users if user is not None and user.enabled]
reference_tests:
import asyncio
from async_directory import active_display_names
from async_user_ports import UserRecord


class Repo:
    def __init__(self, values): self.values = values; self.calls = []
    async def fetch(self, identifier): self.calls.append(identifier); await asyncio.sleep(0); return self.values.get(identifier)


def test_async_repository_contract():
    repo = Repo({"1": UserRecord("1", "Ada"), "2": UserRecord("2", "Bob", False)})
    assert asyncio.run(active_display_names(repo, ["1", "2", "3"])) == ["Ada"]
    assert sorted(repo.calls) == ["1", "2", "3"]


def test_empty_batch():
    assert asyncio.run(active_display_names(Repo({}), [])) == []

import pytest
from managed_query import ManagedQuery
from resource_contracts import QueryLease


class Connection:
    def __init__(self, rows): self.rows = rows; self.statements = []
    def query(self, statement): self.statements.append(statement); return self.rows


class Pool:
    def __init__(self, connection): self.lease = QueryLease("l1", connection); self.releases = []
    def acquire(self): return self.lease
    def release(self, lease, broken): self.releases.append((lease, broken))


def test_context_manager_success():
    connection = Connection([{"id": 1}]); pool = Pool(connection)
    with ManagedQuery(pool, "select 1") as rows: assert rows == [{"id": 1}]
    assert connection.statements == ["select 1"] and pool.releases == [(pool.lease, False)]


def test_context_manager_marks_broken_and_propagates():
    pool = Pool(Connection([]))
    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError):
        with ManagedQuery(pool, "select"): raise RuntimeError("boom")
    assert pool.releases == [(pool.lease, True)]

project_files.log_contracts.py:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum


class Severity(Enum):
    DEBUG = ("DEBUG", 10)
    INFO = ("INFO", 20)
    WARNING = ("WARN", 30)
    ERROR = ("ERROR", 40)
    CRITICAL = ("CRITICAL", 50)

    def __init__(self, label: str, rank: int) -> None:
        self._value_ = label
        self.rank = rank


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class LogEntry:
    severity: Severity
    message: str

reference_tests:
from log_contracts import LogEntry, Severity
from log_generator import iter_alerts


def test_default_and_custom_thresholds():
    entries = [LogEntry(Severity.INFO, "ready"), LogEntry(Severity.ERROR, "bad"), LogEntry(Severity.CRITICAL, "down")]
    assert list(iter_alerts(entries)) == ["[ERROR] bad", "[CRITICAL] down"]
    assert list(iter_alerts(entries, Severity.WARNING)) == ["[ERROR] bad", "[CRITICAL] down"]


def test_is_lazy_generator():
    seen = []
    def source():
        seen.append(1); yield LogEntry(Severity.ERROR, "one")
        seen.append(2); yield LogEntry(Severity.ERROR, "two")
    result = iter_alerts(source()); assert seen == []
    assert next(result) == "[ERROR] one" and seen == [1]
